_calibrationSource: Thresholds calibrated against published pediatric gait norms. See normative-references.json for full citations.

asymmetry._note: Composite frontal asymmetry score (0-1). Mild ≈ 1.5 SD above healthy mean hip asymmetry; significant ≈ clear visible pelvic drop + trunk lean.

irregularRhythm._note: Stride CV. Hausdorff 2007: healthy CV ~0.03; children naturally higher. Mild at 0.08 avoids over-flagging normal pediatric variability.

lateralInstability._note: Normalized trunk sway score (0-1). Old threshold (mild=0.08) flagged too aggressively on normal children. Raised to reduce false positives.

pathDeviation._note: Normalized path deviation score (0-1). Children naturally show some path variation. Old threshold (mild=0.10) was too sensitive.

_stepTimingNote: Symmetry ratio below which step timing contributes to asymmetry concern. Robinson et al. 2006: healthy ~0.97±0.025; old value (0.85) was too lenient and missed genuine asymmetry.
